name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
administered_by: DPIIT, Ministry of Commerce and Industry
domain: Sector-agnostic (preference to social impact, healthcare, agritech, biotech, fintech, education, mobility)
location: Pan-India
funding_amount: Up to ₹20 lakh grant for proof-of-concept/prototype; up to ₹50 lakh debt/convertible for market entry
funding_type: Government Grant — non-dilutive, routed through approved incubators
eligibility: DPIIT-recognized startup
Incorporated not more than 2 years ago at time of application
Indian promoter shareholding above 51%
Not received more than ₹10 lakh in prior government funding
deadline: Rolling — ongoing via incubator partners
required_documents: Pitch deck (problem, solution, market size, business model)
application_process: Apply via seedfund.startupindia.gov.in to up to 3 preferred incubators

name: BIRAC Biotechnology Ignition Grant (BIG)
administered_by: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), DBT
domain: Biotechnology, life sciences, healthcare, agri-biotech, industrial biotech
location: Pan-India
funding_amount: Up to ₹50 lakh
funding_type: Government Grant — non-dilutive, milestone-based disbursement
eligibility: Indian startup or individual entrepreneur
Novel product or technology innovation in biotech or life sciences
Not a purely software or IT product
Team must include someone with relevant scientific background
deadline: Rolling rounds — check birac.nic.in for current call
application_process: Submit application via birac.nic.in during open call windows

name: NIDHI PRAYAS Programme
administered_by: National Science and Technology Entrepreneurship Development Board (NSTEDB), DST
domain: Science and technology-based innovations — hardware, deep tech, science-driven products
location: Pan-India via NIDHI PRAYAS centres
funding_amount: Up to ₹10 lakh
funding_type: Government Grant — non-dilutive, for proof-of-concept and prototyping
eligibility: Indian citizen with a technology product or innovation concept
Should not have previously received NIDHI funding
DPIIT recognition preferred but not always mandatory
Science or engineering background in core innovation
deadline: Cohort-based — periodic calls at participating PRAYAS centres
application_process: Apply via dst.gov.in or nearest NIDHI PRAYAS centre

name: iCreate (International Centre for Entrepreneurship and Technology)
administered_by: Government of India and Government of Gujarat (autonomous body)
domain: Deep tech, manufacturing, IoT, AI and ML, defence, energy, agriculture
location: Ahmedabad, Gujarat (with national reach programmes)
funding_amount: Up to ₹25 lakh (varies by programme)
funding_type: Government Grant + Incubation support — lab space, mentors, prototyping
eligibility: Indian startup or individual innovator
Hardware, deep tech, or advanced manufacturing focus preferred
Must participate in residential or virtual incubation programme
deadline: Cohort-based — periodic open calls
application_process: Apply via icreate.org.in during open cohort windows

name: Atal Innovation Mission (AIM) — Atal Incubation Centres
domain: Sector-agnostic, with focus on technology-driven startups aligned to national priorities
location: Pan-India via AIC network
funding_amount: Equity-free support via AIC incubators (AIC itself receives up to ₹10 crore grant)
funding_type: Government Grant-in-aid to incubators who provide equity-free support to startups
eligibility: Indian startup seeking incubation at an AIM-supported AIC
Innovative product or service concept
Willingness to go through structured incubation programme
deadline: Rolling — apply to individual AIC centres
required_documents: Prototype or MVP evidence if available
application_process: Apply directly to individual AICs listed on aim.gov.in

name: Y Combinator (YC) Batch Programme
domain: Sector-agnostic — software, hardware, biotech, fintech, climate, AI, consumer, B2B SaaS
location: Global applications accepted; programme held in San Francisco
funding_amount: $500,000 for 7% equity (2024 deal: $125k uncapped SAFE + $375k MFN SAFE)
funding_type: Equity investment + Accelerator — 3-month batch, mentorship, Demo Day, alumni network
eligibility: Any startup at any stage globally
Typically 2-4 co-founders preferred
Working prototype or MVP strongly preferred
Founders must commit full-time during the programme
Non-US founders accepted and very common
deadline: Two batches per year (Winter and Summer) — apply at ycombinator.com/apply
required_documents: Online application form — no formal documents required
Links to live product if available
application_process: Apply online at ycombinator.com/apply; shortlisted teams interviewed by YC partners; decisions within weeks

name: Peak XV Partners (Sequoia India) Surge Accelerator
administered_by: Peak XV Partners (formerly Sequoia Capital India)
domain: Consumer internet, B2B SaaS, fintech, healthcare, edtech, deep tech
location: India and Southeast Asia
funding_amount: $1 million to $2 million per company (Surge cohort); follow-on up to $100 million+ from the main fund
funding_type: Equity investment — seed via Surge cohort, Series A and beyond via Peak XV fund
eligibility: Startup based in India or Southeast Asia
Product already in market — MVP or early revenue preferred
Scalable technology-led business model
Strong founding team with domain expertise
deadline: Twice yearly cohorts — check surge.sequoiacap.com for open applications
required_documents: Pitch deck (problem, solution, traction, team, market)
application_process: Apply via surge.sequoiacap.com or pitch to Peak XV partners directly

name: 500 Global (formerly 500 Startups) Accelerator
domain: Sector-agnostic — strong track record in fintech, consumer, marketplace, healthcare, SaaS
location: Global — programmes in US, Asia, MENA, LatAm, Africa
funding_amount: $150,000 for 6% equity (standard deal; varies by programme)
funding_type: Equity investment + 4-month accelerator — mentorship, network, Demo Day
eligibility: Startup at prototype to early revenue stage
Product in the market or close to launch
At least one full-time founder
Global applications accepted
deadline: Rolling cohorts per region — check 500.co/accelerators for current open programmes
required_documents: Key metrics: users, revenue, month-on-month growth
application_process: Apply via 500.co; shortlisted teams proceed to interviews; rolling admission for some programmes

name: Techstars Accelerator Programme
domain: Sector-agnostic — vertical programmes in fintech, health, sustainability, smart city, media, sports
location: Global — 50+ programmes across North America, Europe, Asia, Africa, LatAm
funding_amount: $120,000 for 6% equity (standard; structure varies by programme)
funding_type: Equity investment + 3-month accelerator — mentorship, network, Demo Day, access to $6B+ investor network
eligibility: Pre-seed to seed stage startup
Any geography accepted for most programmes
Sector-specific programmes require relevant domain focus
Founders must relocate for duration of programme
deadline: Rolling cohorts — apply at techstars.com/apply; each programme has its own deadline
required_documents: Online application — no formal documents at initial stage
application_process: Apply at techstars.com/apply; multi-round interviews; decisions per cohort

name: Antler Early-Stage VC
geography: Global — 50+ cities including Bangalore, Singapore, London, NYC, Stockholm
domain: Sector-agnostic — technology-first companies; special focus on AI, climate, health, fintech
location: Global — strong India presence via Bangalore cohort
funding_amount: $250,000 to $500,000 pre-seed for 10-12% equity (varies by city and programme)
funding_type: Equity investment — company builder model; invests before product exists and helps find co-founders
eligibility: Individual founders or early teams — idea stage accepted
Strong domain expertise, technical skills, or relevant background
Commitment to build a full-time venture
Global applications accepted; India programme open to Indian founders
deadline: Rolling cohorts every 6 months per city — apply at antler.co
required_documents: Online application form only at initial stage
Brief description of idea or background — idea not required
References or work portfolio if available
application_process: Apply via antler.co; shortlisted candidates join residency and receive investment decision within 10 weeks

name: Accel India (Accel Partners India)
domain: Consumer internet, SaaS, fintech, healthcare, edtech, deep tech, marketplace
location: India — portfolio includes Flipkart, Swiggy, Freshworks, BrowserStack
funding_amount: $500,000 to $15 million at seed and Series A; up to $100 million+ at growth
funding_type: Equity investment — seed, Series A, and growth rounds
eligibility: India-based startup with demonstrated product-market fit or strong early traction
Scalable technology business model
Strong founding team — ideally 2+ co-founders with complementary skills
Clear path to a large addressable market
deadline: No fixed deadline — rolling investment year-round
required_documents: Pitch deck (10-15 slides: problem, solution, market, traction, team, financials, funding ask)
Key metrics dashboard (ARR, MoM growth, churn, CAC, LTV)
application_process: Email pitch deck to Accel India team or apply via accel.com/india; warm introductions from portfolio founders strongly recommended

name: Nexus Venture Partners
domain: Enterprise SaaS, deep tech, consumer internet, cloud infrastructure, fintech, healthtech
location: India (Bangalore, Mumbai) and Silicon Valley
funding_amount: $500,000 to $10 million at seed and Series A
funding_type: Equity investment — seed and early-stage institutional rounds
eligibility: India-based or India-related startup
Strong technical founding team
Scalable product with early signals of market fit
Defensible technology or business model moat
deadline: No fixed deadline — rolling investment
application_process: Submit via nexusvp.com/contact or via warm introduction from Nexus network

name: Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation Grand Challenges
domain: Global health, agricultural development, financial services for the poor, nutrition, sanitation, vaccines, maternal health
location: Global — strong focus on low- and middle-income countries including India, Africa, SE Asia
funding_amount: $100,000 Phase 1 Exploration grant; up to $1 million+ for Phase 2 if successful
funding_type: Non-dilutive grant — no equity taken
eligibility: Any individual, startup, NGO, or research institution globally
Project must address a defined global health or development challenge
Innovative approach with evidence of feasibility
Must openly share learnings — open IP requirement in some programmes
deadline: Topic-specific rounds with fixed deadlines — check gcgh.grandchallenges.org for current open calls
application_process: Apply via gcgh.grandchallenges.org; Phase 1 is open to all; Phase 2 is invitation-only after Phase 1 review

name: Wellcome Trust Health Innovation Grants
domain: Global health, mental health, infectious disease, biomedical research, science communication, climate and health
location: Global — India is a priority geography
funding_amount: £50,000 to £5 million or more depending on programme
funding_type: Non-dilutive grant — no equity or repayment required
eligibility: Researchers, startups, or organisations working on global health innovation
UK or international applicants accepted
Must align with one of Wellcome's current funding priorities
Collaborative proposals welcomed
deadline: Programme-specific deadlines — check wellcome.org/grant-funding for current opportunities
required_documents: Letter of intent or preliminary application
application_process: Apply via wellcome.org; most schemes require an Expression of Interest before full submission

name: European Innovation Council (EIC) Accelerator
domain: Deep tech, climate, health, digital, manufacturing — high-risk high-impact innovations
location: EU member states and associated countries; non-EU applicants eligible for equity-only component
funding_amount: Up to €2.5 million grant plus up to €15 million equity investment
funding_type: Blended — non-dilutive grant plus optional equity investment by EIC Fund
eligibility: SME or startup from EU member state or EIC-associated country
Breakthrough technology with potential for global market disruption
Innovation beyond current state of the art
Solo or consortium applications accepted
deadline: 3 cut-off dates per year — check eic.ec.europa.eu for current calls
application_process: Apply via eic.ec.europa.eu; Step 1 short application; Step 2 full proposal; Step 3 interview with EIC jury

name: Google for Startups Accelerator India
domain: AI and ML, cloud, healthtech, fintech, edtech, agritech — technology-driven startups
location: India — primarily Bangalore; programme partly virtual
funding_amount: No cash investment — up to $200,000 in Google Cloud credits plus mentorship and tools
funding_type: Non-dilutive accelerator — no equity taken; credits, mentorship, and Google product access
eligibility: India-based startup at seed or Series A stage
Using or planning to use Google Cloud or AI and ML technologies
Product already live with demonstrable traction
deadline: Annual cohort — check developers.google.com/community/accelerator for open applications
required_documents: Description of technical stack and Google tech usage
application_process: Apply via developers.google.com/community/accelerator; shortlisted teams interviewed by Google

name: Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub
domain: Technology-first startups — AI, cloud, SaaS, healthcare IT, fintech, productivity
location: Global — fully remote and virtual programme
funding_amount: Up to $150,000 in Azure cloud credits plus GitHub, Microsoft 365, LinkedIn, and OpenAI credits
funding_type: Non-dilutive — no equity taken; technology credits and tools only
eligibility: Any startup globally at any stage
Must be building on or planning to build on Microsoft or Azure technology
No revenue requirement — idea-stage startups accepted
Free to apply at any time
deadline: No deadline — rolling applications year-round
application_process: Sign up at startups.microsoft.com/en-us/founders-hub; instant tier-based access

name: Amazon AWS Activate Programme
domain: Technology-first startups across all sectors
location: Global — fully online
funding_amount: Up to $100,000 in AWS credits (Founders tier: $1,000; Portfolio tier: up to $100,000 via associated VC or incubator)
funding_type: Non-dilutive — no equity taken; cloud credits and technical support
eligibility: Any startup globally
Founders tier: self-apply with no requirements
Portfolio tier: must be backed by an AWS Activate partner such as a VC, incubator, or accelerator
No revenue threshold required
deadline: Rolling — apply at any time
required_documents: Activating organisation name for Portfolio tier
application_process: Apply at aws.amazon.com/activate; Founders tier approved immediately; Portfolio tier requires partner confirmation

name: Breakthrough Energy Ventures
domain: Climate technology — clean energy, sustainable agriculture, industrial decarbonisation, carbon capture, clean transportation, zero-emission buildings
location: Global — US, Europe, India priority
funding_amount: $1 million to $100 million or more depending on stage and round
funding_type: Equity investment — patient capital with 20-year fund horizon
eligibility: Science-based clean energy or climate startup
Technology must have potential to reduce global greenhouse gas emissions by at least 500 million tons per year
Strong founding team with deep technical expertise
Willing to accept long development timelines typical of deep tech
deadline: No fixed deadline — rolling investment; proactive outreach or inbound referrals
required_documents: Technical white paper or patent filings
application_process: Contact via b-t.energy; warm introductions via existing Breakthrough portfolio strongly preferred

name: Omidyar Network India
domain: Financial inclusion, digital infrastructure, education, governance and citizen engagement, news and media
location: India
funding_amount: $250,000 to $5 million (varies by programme and stage)
funding_type: Equity investment or non-dilutive grant depending on entity type (for-profit vs NGO)
eligibility: India-based organisation — for-profit startup, social enterprise, or NGO
Business or programme that creates measurable social impact
Scalable model with demonstrable beneficiary reach
Openness to impact measurement and reporting
deadline: Rolling — proactive pitching preferred; check omidyar.com for current focus areas
required_documents: Impact thesis and theory of change
Evidence of early traction or pilot results
application_process: Submit via omidyar.com or contact Omidyar India team through their website

name: Villgro Innovations Foundation
domain: Healthcare, agritech, clean energy, livelihood, education — serving low-income communities in India
location: India
funding_amount: ₹15 lakh to ₹1 crore across grant and equity (varies by programme)
funding_type: Grant for early stage plus equity investment for growth stage; incubation support included
eligibility: Social enterprise or impact-first startup serving bottom-of-pyramid population
India-based or India-focused team
Technology or innovation-led solution
At least idea-stage with prototype plan
deadline: Cohort-based — check villgro.org for current programme openings
application_process: Apply via villgro.org/apply during active programme windows

name: NASSCOM 10000 Startups Programme
domain: Technology startups — SaaS, AI and ML, IoT, cybersecurity, cloud, healthtech, edtech, fintech
location: India — multiple city hubs including Bangalore, Hyderabad, Delhi, Mumbai
funding_amount: Non-cash support: co-working space, cloud credits, mentorship, investor connects worth up to ₹20 lakh equivalent
funding_type: Non-dilutive accelerator support — no equity taken by NASSCOM
eligibility: India-registered technology startup
Product or service with some validation — prototype or early customers
Open to B2B and B2C models
Cannot be a services-only company
deadline: Rolling cohorts — apply at 10000startups.com
application_process: Apply at 10000startups.com; shortlisted startups join the programme at a city hub

name: Qualcomm Ventures
domain: Semiconductor, AI and ML, 5G, IoT, automotive tech, robotics, AR and VR, mobile technology
location: Global — India programme very active in Bangalore
funding_amount: $500,000 to $5 million or more at seed and Series A
funding_type: Equity investment — strategic corporate venture aligned with Qualcomm technology roadmap
eligibility: Deep tech or hardware-enabled startup
Technology synergy with Qualcomm chipsets, 5G, AI, or IoT platforms
Strong technical founding team
India and global startups accepted
deadline: Rolling — proactive pitching year-round
required_documents: Patent or IP summary if available
application_process: Submit via qualcommventures.com/contact or via warm introduction from Qualcomm portfolio or employees

name: Salesforce Ventures — Impact Fund
domain: Enterprise SaaS, CRM and cloud technology ecosystem companies, equality, sustainability tech
location: Global — US, Europe, India, APAC
funding_amount: $250,000 to $10 million depending on stage
funding_type: Equity investment — strategic corporate venture; often co-invests with financial VCs
eligibility: Startup building on or complementary to the Salesforce platform or ecosystem
Enterprise SaaS or cloud-based B2B technology company
Demonstrated early customer traction
Strong alignment with Salesforce equality or sustainability goals for Impact Fund
deadline: Rolling — no fixed deadline; proactive pitching
required_documents: Financial model and current ARR and metrics
application_process: Contact via salesforce.com/ventures or via warm introduction from Salesforce employee or portfolio company
